nodes.name: Bernoulli_Probabilistic_Oracle
nodes.description: Defines and evaluates a binary observation model as a pure probabilistic oracle with no persistent mutable state.

inputs.name: x
inputs.constraints: Observed outcome(s), each value in {0,1} when likelihood/log_prob is evaluated

outputs.name: log_prob
outputs.constraints: Elementwise log-likelihood values for the binary observation model

nodes.type_signature: (p: float, x: int | array<int>) -> float | array<float>
nodes.conceptual_summary: Binary_State_LogMass_Oracle. Maps a binary observation and a unit-interval parameter to the log of a two-outcome discrete mass function. The computation is closed-form and elementwise: log_mass = x * log(p) + (1 - x) * log(1 - p), with broadcasting over arrays when provided. It performs no parameter updates or internal caching, so identical inputs always produce identical outputs. Applications: Digital communications: scoring observed bit sequences under a binary noise/channel model. Reliability engineering: evaluating pass/fail component test outcomes against hypothesized failure propensity. Computational biology: modeling binary assay readouts (activation/inactivation) under a latent success-rate parameter.

nodes.name: conjugate_parameter_fold
nodes.description: Analytical closed-form parameter folding via sufficient statistics. Reads observed binary data, computes sum of successes (ys) and count (n), updates hyperparameters: α_post = α_prior + ys, β_post = β_prior + n - ys. Returns updated parameter tuple.

inputs.name: prior
inputs.constraints: Two-element hyperparameter vector (α, β) with positive real components.

outputs.name: updated_params
outputs.constraints: New immutable hyperparameter vector within the same parametric family; inputs are not mutated.

nodes.type_signature: (prior: hyperparameter tuple, data: array<int>) -> hyperparameter tuple
nodes.conceptual_summary: Closed_Form_Parameter_Fold. Reads binary observations, computes sum of successes and sample count as sufficient statistics, then folds them into the prior hyperparameter tuple using exact algebraic addition. The operation is pure: it returns a new hyperparameter tuple without mutating inputs. No sampling or numerical optimisation is required. is_conjugate: true.

edges.description: Observed binary data flows from the probabilistic oracle to the conjugate parameter fold.
